---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{3.3 - Selecting Tests: Practical Aspects}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```
:::

## Practical aspects

Selecting a test is also base on practical (non scientific) aspects



## A tentative list of practical aspects

- Popularity/Clinical diffusion \pause

- Availability (e.g., is the test free?) \pause

- Personal preference


## Three intersecting aspects
\centering
![](Figures/Selecting_Tests.png){width="70%"}

## Practical aspects and Goals

Among the tests with the same goals I would choose those I prefer the most, or that are more widespread

## Practical aspects and Psychometric Properties

Among the tests with _analogous psychometric properties_ I would choose those I prefer the most, or that are more widespread
